contextSummary: Dify, the highest-starred repo in the corpus using Base UI (148k stars). Its dify-ui package wraps essentially every Autocomplete part plus both filtering hooks, with cva size variants and typed grouped/flat item overloads, for the platform's agentic-workflow builder UI.
rankRationale: The single richest Autocomplete wrapper found: it exports both filtering hooks (useFilter and useFilteredItems) alongside 19 visual/structural parts, with typed overloads distinguishing grouped-items (`{items}[]`) from flat-items props at the type level. Coming from the highest-starred adopter in the entire corpus (148k stars) makes this the strongest single 'this is production-grade' citation available. A recomposed story would demo the typed grouped-vs-flat overload split and the exported hooks used standalone (custom filtering UI without the rest of the wrapper). Feeds: prop-level guidance (items shape, useFilter/useFilteredItems), anatomy (full part list), and the real-world-examples header citation.

contextSummary: PostHog's in-house 'Quill' design system primitive. Reuses MenuEmpty/MenuLabel from its own Menu component inside Autocomplete's Empty/GroupLabel, and implements a live 'N results' Status announcer that walks useFilteredItems() output across both flat and grouped item shapes.
rankRationale: PostHog's own 'Quill' primitives layer is the only example found where an Autocomplete wrapper deliberately reuses another component's sub-parts (MenuEmpty, MenuLabel) rather than restyling from scratch, and its AutocompleteStatus hook usage is thoroughly JSDoc'd including the constraint that useFilteredItems throws outside a Root provider. A recomposed story would show the live results counter updating as the user types, and a second story could contrast the flat vs grouped count-summing logic. Feeds: cross-component reuse guidance, the useFilteredItems API section, and live-region/status accessibility guidance.

contextSummary: GitButler's lite web UI implements a command-palette dialog by composing Autocomplete inside Dialog, with ScrollArea for the list; the file header cites Base UI's own docs recipe verbatim.
rankRationale: A well-known Git client (GitButler, 21k stars) implements a command palette by composing three Base UI components together — Dialog for the modal shell, Autocomplete (inline, always open, keepHighlight) for filtering, and ScrollArea for the results list — and its file header literally says it is based on Base UI's own docs recipe for the command-palette pattern. This is the best evidence in the whole corpus that Base UI's own documented composition recipes get adopted verbatim in production. A recomposed story is the command palette itself: open with a keyboard shortcut, type to filter grouped results, arrow/select, Enter to activate. Feeds: composition handbook (Autocomplete + Dialog + ScrollArea), the command-palette use-case section, and inline/always-open mode guidance.

contextSummary: reui ships parallel 'base' (Base UI) and 'radix' registry variants of every component; its Base UI Autocomplete is the fullest anatomy found (Backdrop + Arrow), compound-styled for five simultaneous visual skins.
rankRationale: reui is architecturally unique in this corpus: it ships parallel Base UI ('base') and Radix ('radix') implementations of the same component side by side, letting consumers choose their primitive layer, and its Autocomplete uses the fullest anatomy found for this component (Backdrop + Arrow, matching the fullest Select anatomy pattern seen elsewhere), with every class list compound-styled to support five simultaneous visual skins (`style-vega/maia/nova/lyra/mira`) toggled by an ancestor class rather than a separate theme file. A recomposed story is an anatomy tour with Backdrop/Arrow visible, or a skin-switcher story cycling the five styles live. Feeds: anatomy (Backdrop/Arrow), multi-brand theming architecture guidance, and the Radix-migration/parity narrative.

contextSummary: WordPress' new @wordpress/ui form-primitives package wraps Autocomplete one file per part, sharing structure with its sibling Combobox and Select primitives inside the Block Editor monorepo.
rankRationale: WordPress' new @wordpress/ui package decomposes Autocomplete into one file per part (root/input/list/popup/group/value/clear/empty), the cleanest anatomical breakdown in the corpus, mirroring the same per-part convention already documented for Select and Field from the same package — useful as a single citation that establishes 'this is how a framework-scale team organizes a Base UI wrapper.' Feeds: anatomy documentation style, and the per-part composition pattern cross-referenced from Field/Select/Combobox.

contextSummary: Typebot, an open-source chatbot/form builder. Exposes Autocomplete as a single namespace object rather than named exports — a distinct minimal API-surface style, inside a product whose purpose is building forms.
rankRationale: Typebot bundles Autocomplete as `export const Autocomplete = { Root, Input, List, Item, Popup }` — an object-namespace API distinct from every other candidate's named-export style — inside a form-building product where the choice of API surface for form controls is directly relevant to readers evaluating API ergonomics. Feeds: API-style comparison note (named exports vs namespace object) and the form-builder-adjacent real-world context.

contextSummary: Cloudflare's official component library. Autocomplete takes a top-level label/required/description/error prop set and auto-wraps in the same Field component every Kumo control uses, with JSDoc explicitly distinguishing it from Combobox.
rankRationale: Cloudflare's Kumo library integrates Field directly into Autocomplete's own props (`label`, `required`, `description`, `error` all flow to an internal `<Field>` wrap) rather than requiring the consumer to compose Field manually, and its JSDoc states outright: 'Unlike Combobox, the input value is not constrained to the suggestion list items' — first-party evidence for the doc's select-vs-combobox-vs-autocomplete decision boundary. A recomposed story is a labeled, field-wrapped Autocomplete with an inline validation error. Feeds: when-to-use / when-not-to-use (autocomplete vs combobox), Field-integration composition pattern, a11y/labeling.

contextSummary: 9ui's registry demos cover the widest spread of Autocomplete use cases found anywhere: async-loaded items, a virtualized long list, grouped items, a custom fuzzy-matcher, and an inline mode, all built on the same wrapper.

attempts:
open-state interaction failed: locator.click: Timeout 3000ms exceeded.
Call log:
  - waiting for getByText('Accordion').first()

rankRationale: No other candidate has this many distinct worked use-case demos for one component: async-loaded items (loading state while fetching), a virtualized long list, grouped items, a custom fuzzy-matcher (replacing the default substring filter), an inline (always-open, no popup) mode, and a limited-results variant. Each is a small, focused file ideal as a direct story-recomposition source. Feeds: async-loading pattern, virtualization guidance, custom filterFn/fuzzy-matching prop, and the inline-mode variant story.

contextSummary: lumi-ui's autocomplete-form.tsx composes Autocomplete inside Field inside Form in a single real submission flow — a rare file that exercises all three of this session's Tier-1 targets together.
rankRationale: The only file in the whole session's research that puts Autocomplete, Field, and Form together in one real submission flow: `<Form onFormSubmit={...}><Field name="framework"><Autocomplete items={tags} required>...</Autocomplete><FieldError/></Field></Form>`. This is the single best source for a cross-component 'form field with an Autocomplete control' story, and directly demonstrates required-field validation surfacing through FieldError. Feeds: form-integration section for all three components, and the required-field validation story.

contextSummary: Selia component library. A compact cva-driven Autocomplete wrapper (default/subtle/plain input variants) with a live docs site and example page.
rankRationale: A smaller, plainer wrapper than the others, but useful as the 'minimal viable Autocomplete' baseline — three cva input-visual variants (default/subtle/plain), a live browsable docs site, and permissive MIT licensing make it a safe, simple reference implementation to point beginners at before showing the richer wrappers. Feeds: getting-started composition example.
